hooks.prompt:
A Bash command is about to run. Check $ARGUMENTS for dangerous patterns:
- rm -rf / or ~ or .
- sudo rm
- curl|wget piped to sh/bash
- git push --force
- git reset --hard
- DROP DATABASE/TABLE
- chmod -R 777
- Production deploys (vercel --prod, railway deploy, npm publish, fly deploy, heroku push, aws deploy, gcloud deploy)

If dangerous: {"ok": false, "reason": "Blocked: <pattern>. Requires manual approval."}
If production deploy: {"ok": true, "reason": "Production deployment detected. Confirm with user before proceeding."}
Otherwise: {"ok": true}

IBR (Interface Built Right) is installed — a design validation tool that provides structured UI data (computed CSS, element bounds, handler wiring, accessibility). A Playwright screenshot/snapshot is being called. Screenshots are useful for visual coherence, rendering bugs, and canvas/SVG content. For precise property verification (exact CSS values, handler detection, a11y), also run an IBR scan. The best validation combines both. Respond: {"ok": true, "reason": "Tip: Also run npx ibr scan <url> --json for exact CSS values, handler detection, and a11y data. Scan + screenshot together gives the most complete validation."}

Check $ARGUMENTS for the file path. If it's a UI file (.tsx, .jsx, .vue, .svelte, .css, .scss, or in components/, pages/, app/ directories), respond: {"ok": true, "reason": "UI file detected. After changes, validate with IBR: npx ibr scan <url> --json to verify implementation matches user intent."}. If it targets a sensitive path (/etc/, /usr/, ~/.ssh/, ~/.aws/, ~/.gnupg/, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.gitconfig), respond: {"ok": false, "reason": "Write to sensitive system path blocked."}. Otherwise: {"ok": true}

A file was just edited. Check $ARGUMENTS for the file path. If it's a UI file (.tsx, .jsx, .vue, .svelte, .css, .scss), respond: {"ok": true, "reason": "UI file modified. Validate with: npx ibr scan <url> --json to verify changes match user intent."}. Otherwise: {"ok": true}

Session ending. Check $ARGUMENTS for evidence of UI work (.tsx/.jsx/.vue/.svelte edits, UI keywords). If UI work was done, check if IBR was used (look for 'npx ibr', 'ibr scan', 'Element Audit', 'session:screenshot'). If UI work done but IBR NOT used: {"ok": true, "reason": "UI changes detected but not validated. Consider: npx ibr scan <url> --json to verify implementation."}. Otherwise: {"ok": true}

IBR (Interface Built Right) is active — your design validation tool. IBR verifies UI implementation matches user intent by extracting structured data (computed CSS, handler wiring, accessibility, page structure). Use npx ibr scan <url> --json after building UI to validate, npx ibr memory to store design specs. Respond: {"ok": true, "reason": "IBR ready. Primary: npx ibr scan <url> --json to validate UI. Memory: npx ibr memory add to store design specs."}
